dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - May 10, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**SIZZLING OFFENSES AND DOMINANT PITCHING DISPLAYS ACROSS MLB**

Yesterday's slate of games saw some of the top performers in the league shine through. In the Miami Marlins' 5-2 victory over the Washington Nationals, Heriberto Hernández led the way with a pair of hits, two RBIs, and a crucial single that scored two runs. Meanwhile, Calvin Faucher picked up the win for the Marlins.

On the pitching side, Andrew Nardi impressed with 2 strikeouts in just 0.2 innings of work. Elsewhere, the Los Angeles Angels' José Soriano turned in an outstanding performance, throwing 7.2 innings and striking out 7 in the Angels' 6-1 win over the Toronto Blue Jays. Jo Adell also homered twice for the Angels.

In other notable performances, Kyle Schwarber hit two home runs and drove in two runs for the Philadelphia Phillies in their 6-0 win over the Colorado Rockies. Cristopher Sánchez earned the win for the Phillies, striking out 7 in 7 innings. And in the Houston Astros' 5-0 loss to the Cincinnati Reds, Spencer Steer homered for the Reds.

In the Minnesota Twins' 5-4 win over the Cleveland Guardians, Chase DeLauter had two hits and two RBIs, while Kendry Rojas earned the win with 3.1 scoreless innings of relief. Logan Gilbert struck out 9 in 6 innings for the Seattle Mariners in their 2-1 loss to the Chicago White Sox.

Finally, Jacob deGrom pitched a gem for the Texas Rangers, striking out 10 in 7 innings in their 3-0 win over the Chicago Cubs.
